skill_summary.name: yao-deepseek-crawler
skill_summary.title: Yao DeepSeek Crawler
skill_summary.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
skill_summary.core_value: 把一次性经验沉淀为可复用、可评估、可迁移的 Skill 包体。
skill_summary.audience: Skill 作者、复用团队和后续 reviewer。

completeness_score.label: 完整度
reasons: SKILL.md 已存在，是 Skill 的入口。
README.md 已存在，便于人工阅读。
agents/interface.yaml 已存在，便于跨平台适配。
manifest.json 已存在，生命周期信息可追踪。
reports/ 已存在，生成证据可以随包体迁移。

trigger_score.label: 触发清晰
reasons: frontmatter description 已存在，具备基础路由面。
description 有足够长度说明任务边界。
description 已包含使用场景或排除边界信号。
evals/ 证据不足，误触发检查仍偏弱。
intent-confidence 报告已生成，可辅助判断触发稳定性。

evidence_score.label: 证据充分
reasons: 已生成 12 / 20 类报告证据。
skill-ir.json 已存在。
compiled_targets.json 已存在。
intent-dialogue.json 已存在。
证据不足：缺少 output_quality_scorecard.json, conformance_matrix.json, security_trust_report.json。

maintainability_score.label: 可维护性
reasons: SKILL.md 约 285 个词/字。
入口文件保持克制，可维护性较好。
references/ 证据不足，长指导可能堆在入口。
scripts/ 证据不足，重复执行逻辑可能仍靠人工。
evals/ 证据不足。

portability_score.label: 可迁移性
reasons: agents/interface.yaml 已存在。
manifest.json 已存在。
目标平台或 adapter target 已声明。
入口文件未发现明显私有绝对路径。

context_cost.label: 上下文成本
reasons: 入口约 285 个词/字，references 约 0 个词/字。
分数越高代表上下文成本越低。
上下文成本处于可控区间。

trigger.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.

inputs: A list of questions, per-question repeat count, OpenCLI Browser Bridge profile, optional target brand list, optional crawler script path.
outputs: A canonical JSON dataset, a metrics summary JSON, and a Kami-styled HTML report with brand ranking probabilities, source/channel/title analysis, charts, and conclusions.
结构化 Skill 目录，共 7 类关键资产。
should_trigger: 团队复用前，需要明确触发边界、质量证据和维护责任。

用户说出类似需求时：Use $yao-deepseek-crawler when you need to Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
已有原始资料，但还没有清晰输入、输出和后续迭代路径。
should_not_trigger: 只需要一次性回答、没有复用价值的临时请求。
要求直接执行相邻任务，而不是沉淀或使用这个 Skill。
缺少必要事实且用户不允许澄清的场景。

Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.

boundary_cards.label: Adjacent
boundary_cards.body: 相邻任务需要先确认是否应转为独立 Skill。

boundary_cards.label: Excluded
boundary_cards.body: 不替代人工事实核查，也不静默扩大职责。

strengths: 触发面保持精简，并锚定在 frontmatter description。
已生成 Skill IR，核心语义可先于平台打包被审查和迁移。
已生成目标编译报告，可审查 IR 到 OpenAI、Claude、generic 等目标契约的映射。
已生成 Adoption Drift Report，可把本地使用反馈转为下一轮迭代信号。
已生成 Review Waivers 台账，可记录 reviewer 对 warning 风险的批准、理由和到期时间。
已生成 Review Annotations 台账，可把 reviewer 批注挂到 gate、文件和行号。
gaps: 证据充分需要补强：已生成 12 / 20 类报告证据。
可维护性需要补强：SKILL.md 约 285 个词/字。
recommendations: 先改触发边界，再扩展工作流。
只把重复且稳定的步骤沉淀为脚本。
每次升级后重新生成报告并检查分数原因。

Mark destructive, networked, or external side-effect operations.
Prefer the smallest runnable snippet over broad framework scaffolding.
Primary prompt task family: Creative generation.
Complexity: expert — multiple task families plus governance, evaluation, or expert-level constraints

Stability: system-ready (99/100).

risk_families.label: Tone and specificity
risks: Headings and summaries can drift into generic, interchangeable language.
The output can sound polished but lose the user's actual taste, audience, or scenario.
Strong claims can appear without examples or constraints.
constraints: Anchor titles and summaries in the user's audience, object, and concrete outcome.
Avoid placeholder phrases such as comprehensive guide, ultimate solution, or key insights unless the source demands them.
Preserve one distinctive phrase, constraint, or standard from the user's input.
self_repair: Replace generic title candidates with scenario-specific alternatives.
Delete any polished sentence that could fit almost any project unchanged.

risk_families.label: Markdown readability
risks: Tables can render as dense grids with weak hierarchy or poor mobile readability.
Long bullets can make the output look complete while hiding the actual decision logic.
Mixed heading levels can reduce scanability.
constraints: Use tables only when comparison is the main job; otherwise prefer compact cards or grouped bullets.
Keep table cells short and move explanations below the table.
Use heading levels consistently and keep each section anchored to a user-facing outcome.
self_repair: Preview whether each table still reads well when columns are narrow.
Convert any table with paragraph-length cells into bullets or cards.

risk_families.label: Citation and footnote clutter
risks: Footnote markers or dense citation notes can interrupt the reading flow.
Evidence can be over-attached to obvious statements and under-attached to risky claims.
Source notes may become more prominent than the tutorial itself.
constraints: Attach citations only to claims that need evidence, not to every sentence.
Group source notes at the end of a section when inline markers would hurt readability.
Keep the main sentence readable without requiring the reader to chase a footnote.
self_repair: Remove decorative citations that do not support a material claim.
Move repeated source explanations into one compact source note.

risk_families.label: Screenshot and visual capture
risks: Screenshots can be captured from the wrong state, wrong viewport, or wrong crop.
Missing screenshots can cause the skill to invent visual references instead of declaring the gap.
Image descriptions can omit the action-relevant region.
constraints: Never invent a screenshot; state when visual evidence is missing.
Record the source, viewport, and crop intent for any screenshot-dependent output.
Describe what the reader should inspect in the image, not just that an image exists.
self_repair: Check that every screenshot reference points to a real provided or generated asset.
Reword any visual instruction that depends on an unseen screen state.

risk_families.label: Code and command safety
risks: Commands can omit environment assumptions, working directory, or rollback notes.
Code snippets can look runnable while missing required inputs.
Error handling can be either absent or over-engineered.
constraints: Name the working directory, required inputs, and expected output for each command.
Mark destructive or external side-effect operations explicitly.
Prefer the smallest runnable snippet over broad framework code.
self_repair: Scan each command for cwd, input, output, and side-effect assumptions.
Remove speculative error handling that is not tied to a real failure mode.

human_judgment_boundary: Ask one focused clarification when the real job, output, or exclusion boundary is unclear.
Escalate visible tradeoffs when benchmark patterns conflict with local privacy, naming, or governance constraints.
Do not silently broaden the skill into adjacent jobs just because the examples are nearby.

world_class_readiness.conclusion_zh: 未生成 world-class ledger；当前报告不会宣称世界级完成。
world_class_readiness.conclusion_en: No world-class ledger was generated; this report does not claim world-class completion.

items.title: Tighten trigger and exclusions
items.why: The package needs clearer near-neighbor exclusions before it grows.
actions: Add 3 to 5 should-trigger and should-not-trigger examples.
Refine the frontmatter description to name the recurring job and non-goals.
Run a first trigger evaluation pass before expanding the package.
items.unlocks: Cleaner routing and fewer accidental activations.

items.title: Add the first execution asset
items.why: The package is still mostly prose. Add one asset that removes repeated manual work.
actions: Move stable procedural guidance into references if users will need it repeatedly.
Create one deterministic helper script if a repeated step can be executed instead of described.
Keep the main SKILL.md compact and route-oriented.
items.unlocks: Stronger execution quality without bloating the entrypoint.

items.title: Deepen operator guidance
items.why: The workflow is still thin and may not be repeatable by another operator.
actions: Split the job into distinct phases or checkpoints.
Add success conditions and failure boundaries for each phase.
Link outputs to the step that produces them.
items.unlocks: Better repeatability and easier human review.

introduction: 这份报告用于快速理解新生成 Skill 的定位、原理、触发边界和交付内容。
先确认重复任务、真实输入形态和可交付输出，再决定是否继续加 references、scripts 或 evals。
如果需求仍然模糊，优先回到 intent dialogue 收紧边界，再扩展包体结构。

targets.native_surface: OpenAI-style interface metadata plus neutral Agent Skills source
unsupported_features: client-native script permission prompts are represented as permission contract metadata

targets.native_surface: Claude-compatible neutral source folder with adapter notes
unsupported_features: vendor-native metadata fields are carried as adapter JSON and README notes

targets.native_surface: VS Code/Copilot Agent Skills project or user scope
unsupported_features: VS Code installation scope is documented but not installed by this compiler

next_iteration_candidates.recommendation: Start with a small metadata-only sample before using telemetry for release decisions.

synthesis_highlights: Borrow progressive disclosure: keep the entrypoint lean and move depth into references or scripts.
Borrow a small hypothesis-test-learn loop so the first revision is evidence-backed.
Borrow the habit of designing from the required hand-back output backwards.

highlights: Execution-focused technical artifact with environment assumptions, copyable commands, expected outputs, and side effects made explicit.
Name the working directory and required inputs for commands.
Mark destructive, networked, or external side-effect operations.
Prefer the smallest runnable snippet over broad framework scaffolding.
Primary prompt task family: Creative generation.
Complexity: expert — multiple task families plus governance, evaluation, or expert-level constraints
Completeness: 100/100.
Clarity: 90/100.
Stability: system-ready (99/100).
Owned job: Run multiple DeepSeek web AI-search queries repeatedly, normalize the crawl records, and turn them into GEO probability evidence.
Leverage: Tune the frontmatter description — Name the recurring job, expected input, output, and strongest non-goal in compact language.
Leverage: Install output self-repair checks — Add only the checks that prevent recurring output mistakes.

strengths: The recurring job is concrete enough to anchor the package.
intent_confidence.anchor_sentence: Run multiple DeepSeek web AI-search queries repeatedly, normalize the crawl records, and turn them into GEO probability evidence. Primary output: A canonical JSON dataset, a metrics summary JSON, and a Kami-styled HTML report with brand ranking probabilities, source/channel/title analysis, charts, and conclusions.. Exclusions: Do not handle generic website crawling, DeepSeek API-only calls, unrelated SEO reports, or one-off question answering..
intent_confidence.recommended_action: Intent is clear enough to package the first routeable version.

context.job: Run multiple DeepSeek web AI-search queries repeatedly, normalize the crawl records, and turn them into GEO probability evidence.
real_inputs: A list of questions, per-question repeat count, OpenCLI Browser Bridge profile, optional target brand list, optional crawler script path.
context.primary_output: A canonical JSON dataset, a metrics summary JSON, and a Kami-styled HTML report with brand ranking probabilities, source/channel/title analysis, charts, and conclusions.
context.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
exclusions: Do not handle generic website crawling, DeepSeek API-only calls, unrelated SEO reports, or one-off question answering.
constraints: Local-first; reuse existing opencli-boss-ai DeepSeek browser crawler when available; preserve raw evidence and avoid fabricating brand/source claims.
standards: Every run must expose query count, repeat count, completed sample count, answer/reference totals, brand Top 1 and Top 3 rates, source/domain/channel distribution, and title features.

intent.title: Yao DeepSeek Crawler
intent.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
intent.opening_frame: Let's start from the real work, the result you care about, and the standards that matter here. We can make the structure clearer after that.
intent.reference_note: If you already have examples you admire, bring them in. We will learn the pattern, not copy the source.
conversation_path: Start with the user's own words, not package vocabulary.
Reflect the job, output, and non-goals back in one clean sentence.
Only then offer a tiny scaffold if it would help the user move faster.

opening_styles.label: 温柔陪伴型
opening_styles.best_when: 用户想法还散、还在试探，或者需要先被接住。
opening_styles.message: 我们先不急着把它说成一个很完整的 skill。你就像跟我聊天一样，先说说你最想让它以后稳稳接住哪类重复工作；如果它做得很理想，最后应该交回你一个什么结果。

opening_styles.label: 专业教练型
opening_styles.best_when: 用户目标比较明确，希望被高效带着走。
opening_styles.message: 我们先把这件事讲清楚，再决定 skill 怎么设计。你先告诉我三件事：它要接住的重复任务是什么，别人通常会给它什么材料，最后你希望它交付什么结果。

opening_styles.label: 共创伙伴型
opening_styles.best_when: 用户已经有一些想法，希望一起打磨，不想被填表。
opening_styles.message: 我们把这次当成一次共创。你先给我一个粗糙版本就行，我先帮你看它真正的核心任务是什么，再一起决定边界、结构和接下来最值的一步。

optional_scaffold: The repeated job it should reliably handle
The real inputs people will hand to it
The useful output it should hand back

questions.question: If this skill worked beautifully, what recurring job would it reliably handle for the user every time?
questions.why: This reveals the real job-to-be-done and gives the package a humane center instead of a guessed prompt shape.

questions.question: When someone reaches for this skill in the real world, what materials will they actually hand to it?
questions.why: Input shape decides whether references, scripts, or templates are needed.

questions.question: What finished output should it hand back so the user can immediately keep moving?
questions.why: Outputs should drive the package structure before extra guidance is added.

questions.question: Which nearby requests should this skill politely refuse so the boundary stays clean?
questions.why: The exclusion list is the fastest route to better trigger quality.

questions.question: What matters most here: speed, consistency, auditability, portability, governance, or tone/style fit?
questions.why: Constraints decide how much structure, packaging, and review the skill actually needs.

questions.question: Do you already have any references you want this skill to learn from, such as a repo, product, page, workflow, or prompt example?
questions.why: A good reference can raise the quality bar quickly, but the skill should only borrow patterns and standards, never copy wording or confidential material.

questions.question: What repeated manual step should become a deterministic asset first?
questions.why: This usually reveals whether a script or reference should be created next.

output.capability_sentence: Yao DeepSeek Crawler should turn a recurring request into a reliable reusable output without widening the boundary unnecessarily.

Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.

scan_focus.label: Execution pattern
scan_focus.reason: There is deterministic logic in scripts, so compare how strong references separate prose from executable steps.

scan_focus.label: Portability pattern
scan_focus.reason: The package carries neutral metadata, so scan how good references preserve semantics across targets without forking source.

scan_focus.label: Method pattern
scan_focus.reason: Use the core job description as the anchor for comparison: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.

borrow_plan: External benchmark first: let high-quality public references define the upper bound for method, structure, execution, or portability.
User references second: use them to understand taste, standards, and directional preferences without copying source phrasing.
Local fit third: use local assets only to detect naming conflicts, private dependencies, or compatibility constraints.
Borrow patterns, not prose: extract loops, boundaries, metadata, and operator flow without copying source-specific language.
Keep the package light: reject any borrowed pattern that increases context cost faster than it increases reliability.
reference.priority_rule: External benchmark objects set the pattern ceiling. User references refine taste and standards. Local files only calibrate fit, risk, and compatibility.
non_goals: Do not copy source prose or branding into the new skill.
Do not import gates that cost more context than they save.
Do not use benchmark scanning to justify scope creep.
Do not let local historical habits outrank stronger public benchmarks.

summary.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.
summary.selection_rule: Pick the three smallest next steps that increase reliability more than they increase context cost.
summary.recommended_now_why: The package needs clearer near-neighbor exclusions before it grows.

directions.why: The package needs clearer near-neighbor exclusions before it grows.
actions: Add 3 to 5 should-trigger and should-not-trigger examples.
Refine the frontmatter description to name the recurring job and non-goals.
Run a first trigger evaluation pass before expanding the package.
directions.unlocks: Cleaner routing and fewer accidental activations.
directions.wait_on: Wait to add broader structure until this move clearly improves reliability.

actions: Move stable procedural guidance into references if users will need it repeatedly.
Create one deterministic helper script if a repeated step can be executed instead of described.

directions.do_now: Do this after the first move lands cleanly.

directions.title: Deepen operator guidance
directions.why: The workflow is still thin and may not be repeatable by another operator.
actions: Split the job into distinct phases or checkpoints.
Add success conditions and failure boundaries for each phase.
Link outputs to the step that produces them.
directions.unlocks: Better repeatability and easier human review.
directions.do_now: Do this after the first move lands cleanly.
directions.wait_on: Wait until the package has evidence that this extra structure is justified.

reference_synthesis.description: Use when a user needs to run repeated DeepSeek web AI-search crawls for multiple questions, aggregate probabilistic brand/source/ranking evidence, and generate a JSON dataset plus a Kami-styled HTML analysis report. Not for generic web scraping, DeepSeek API chat, or one-off answer writing.

source_tracks.name: Official skill anatomy and context discipline
source_tracks.borrow: Borrow progressive disclosure: keep the entrypoint lean and move depth into references or scripts.
source_tracks.avoid: Do not let packaging or platform concerns swallow the core job boundary.

source_tracks.name: Hypothesis-test-learn loop
source_tracks.borrow: Borrow a small hypothesis-test-learn loop so the first revision is evidence-backed.
source_tracks.avoid: Do not create experimental overhead that exceeds the skill's real risk tier.

source_tracks.name: Outcome-backwards design
source_tracks.borrow: Borrow the habit of designing from the required hand-back output backwards.
source_tracks.avoid: Do not start with architecture terms before the deliverable is concrete.

reasons: design_conflict
visibility.user_note: Surface the recommendation because intent is still settling or there is a real design conflict that needs a user call.
visibility.reviewer_note: Keep the full benchmark and synthesis evidence visible for authors and reviewers.

synthesis.decision_prompt: Use the recommendation by default. Only surface the underlying benchmark tradeoffs when intent is uncertain or a real design conflict needs a deliberate call.

risk_families.label: Tone and specificity
risks: Headings and summaries can drift into generic, interchangeable language.
The output can sound polished but lose the user's actual taste, audience, or scenario.
Strong claims can appear without examples or constraints.
constraints: Anchor titles and summaries in the user's audience, object, and concrete outcome.
Avoid placeholder phrases such as comprehensive guide, ultimate solution, or key insights unless the source demands them.
Preserve one distinctive phrase, constraint, or standard from the user's input.
self_repair: Replace generic title candidates with scenario-specific alternatives.
Delete any polished sentence that could fit almost any project unchanged.

risk_families.label: Markdown readability
risks: Tables can render as dense grids with weak hierarchy or poor mobile readability.
Long bullets can make the output look complete while hiding the actual decision logic.
Mixed heading levels can reduce scanability.
constraints: Use tables only when comparison is the main job; otherwise prefer compact cards or grouped bullets.
Keep table cells short and move explanations below the table.
Use heading levels consistently and keep each section anchored to a user-facing outcome.
self_repair: Preview whether each table still reads well when columns are narrow.
Convert any table with paragraph-length cells into bullets or cards.

risk_families.label: Citation and footnote clutter
risks: Footnote markers or dense citation notes can interrupt the reading flow.
Evidence can be over-attached to obvious statements and under-attached to risky claims.
Source notes may become more prominent than the tutorial itself.
constraints: Attach citations only to claims that need evidence, not to every sentence.
Group source notes at the end of a section when inline markers would hurt readability.
Keep the main sentence readable without requiring the reader to chase a footnote.
self_repair: Remove decorative citations that do not support a material claim.
Move repeated source explanations into one compact source note.

risk_families.label: Screenshot and visual capture
risks: Screenshots can be captured from the wrong state, wrong viewport, or wrong crop.
Missing screenshots can cause the skill to invent visual references instead of declaring the gap.
Image descriptions can omit the action-relevant region.
constraints: Never invent a screenshot; state when visual evidence is missing.
Record the source, viewport, and crop intent for any screenshot-dependent output.
Describe what the reader should inspect in the image, not just that an image exists.
self_repair: Check that every screenshot reference points to a real provided or generated asset.
Reword any visual instruction that depends on an unseen screen state.

risk_families.label: Code and command safety
risks: Commands can omit environment assumptions, working directory, or rollback notes.
Code snippets can look runnable while missing required inputs.
Error handling can be either absent or over-engineered.
constraints: Name the working directory, required inputs, and expected output for each command.
Mark destructive or external side-effect operations explicitly.
Prefer the smallest runnable snippet over broad framework code.
self_repair: Scan each command for cwd, input, output, and side-effect assumptions.
Remove speculative error handling that is not tied to a real failure mode.

top_risks: Headings and summaries can drift into generic, interchangeable language.
The output can sound polished but lose the user's actual taste, audience, or scenario.
Tables can render as dense grids with weak hierarchy or poor mobile readability.
Long bullets can make the output look complete while hiding the actual decision logic.
Footnote markers or dense citation notes can interrupt the reading flow.
Evidence can be over-attached to obvious statements and under-attached to risky claims.
output_constraints: Anchor titles and summaries in the user's audience, object, and concrete outcome.
Avoid placeholder phrases such as comprehensive guide, ultimate solution, or key insights unless the source demands them.
Use tables only when comparison is the main job; otherwise prefer compact cards or grouped bullets.
Keep table cells short and move explanations below the table.
Attach citations only to claims that need evidence, not to every sentence.
Group source notes at the end of a section when inline markers would hurt readability.
self_repair_checks: Replace generic title candidates with scenario-specific alternatives.
Delete any polished sentence that could fit almost any project unchanged.
Preview whether each table still reads well when columns are narrow.
Convert any table with paragraph-length cells into bullets or cards.
Remove decorative citations that do not support a material claim.
Move repeated source explanations into one compact source note.
output_risk.reviewer_note: Use this report before deepening the package and again before approving example outputs.

primary_artifact.label: Code, CLI, or implementation guide
primary_artifact.direction: Execution-focused technical artifact with environment assumptions, copyable commands, expected outputs, and side effects made explicit.

artifact_families.label: Code, CLI, or implementation guide
artifact_families.direction: Execution-focused technical artifact with environment assumptions, copyable commands, expected outputs, and side effects made explicit.

artifact_families.label: Dashboard or metrics page
artifact_families.direction: Metric-first dashboard with stable dimensions, short labels, visible deltas, and narrative callouts only where they change interpretation.

artifact_families.label: Report or brief
artifact_families.direction: High-trust editorial report with a clear first-screen thesis, compact evidence blocks, and decisions separated from supporting detail.

artifact_families.label: Tutorial or guide
artifact_families.direction: Progressive instructional layout with domain-specific section names, short success checks, and examples close to the user's real input.

artifact_families.label: Review viewer
artifact_families.direction: Side-by-side reviewer studio with explicit tradeoffs, evidence readiness, and fast paths for approving, blocking, or requesting one focused fix.

type: Use a distinctive display face or serif for major claims when the artifact is editorial.
Use a restrained sans for dense body text and technical details.
Use mono only for metadata, paths, commands, labels, and evidence tags.
color: Choose colors from the artifact's domain, brand, or evidence mood.
Do not default to Kami parchment, purple gradients, or generic SaaS blue unless the content justifies it.
Keep accent color limited to decisions, active states, risk, or section anchors.
spacing: Prefer clear grid rhythm over floating decorative cards.
Increase whitespace around decisions and shrink whitespace around supporting metadata.
Split dense content instead of shrinking type or adding scroll traps.
components: Use cards for grouped evidence, tables for comparisons, callouts for decisions, and timelines for sequence.
Keep reviewer-only detail visible but visually quieter than user-facing guidance.

quality_gates: Name the working directory and required inputs for commands.
Mark destructive, networked, or external side-effect operations.
Prefer the smallest runnable snippet over broad framework scaffolding.
Keep charts tied to one analytical question each.
Preserve stable color meaning across metrics and entities.
Keep the first screen useful without requiring the reader to parse every detail.
Use tables only for comparisons; move explanations below the table.
anti_patterns: Do not copy Kami's fixed parchment background as a default.
Do not use generic purple gradients, glass cards, or stock SaaS hero sections unless the content calls for them.
Do not let Markdown tables become the default shape for every comparison or explanation.
Do not turn reviewer evidence into user-facing clutter.
Do not invent screenshots, citations, charts, or UI states.
artifact_design.reviewer_note: Use this profile to judge whether the generated artifacts feel designed for their job, not merely rendered.

task_families.label: Creative generation
task_families.role_guidance: Use a taste-aware creator role with clear audience, tone, and originality boundaries.
task_families.task_guidance: Generate variants, explain selection logic, and preserve the user's distinctive constraints.
task_families.format_guidance: Return options with rationale, selection criteria, and refinement paths.

task_families.label: Prompt engineering
task_families.role_guidance: Use a prompt engineer role only when role design materially improves execution.
task_families.task_guidance: Map Role, Task, and Format into skill behavior rather than copying a large prompt template.
task_families.format_guidance: Return a compact prompt contract plus tests, quality matrix, and usage notes.

task_families.label: Execution operation
task_families.role_guidance: Use an operator role with explicit boundaries, inputs, outputs, and failure handling.
task_families.task_guidance: Convert the job into ordered steps with validation checks and stop conditions.
task_families.format_guidance: Return a runbook-like handoff with commands, checks, owners, and next actions when relevant.

task_families.label: Dialogue interaction
task_families.role_guidance: Use a conversational role that asks only high-leverage questions and remembers the user's goal.
task_families.task_guidance: Clarify intent, resolve uncertainty, and converge toward a recommendation instead of a long option list.
task_families.format_guidance: Return concise prompts, decision points, and reviewer-visible assumptions.

complexity.reason: multiple task families plus governance, evaluation, or expert-level constraints

need_model.explicit_need: Run multiple DeepSeek web AI-search queries repeatedly, normalize the crawl records, and turn them into GEO probability evidence.
need_model.implicit_need: The reusable skill needs a stable role, task, and output contract rather than a one-off prompt.
need_model.scenario: A list of questions, per-question repeat count, OpenCLI Browser Bridge profile, optional target brand list, optional crawler script path.
need_model.user_level: infer from examples and standards; ask only if it changes output depth
need_model.success_standard: Every run must expose query count, repeat count, completed sample count, answer/reference totals, brand Top 1 and Top 3 rates, source/domain/channel distribution, and title features.

rtf_to_skill.role: Use a taste-aware creator role with clear audience, tone, and originality boundaries.
rtf_to_skill.task: Generate variants, explain selection logic, and preserve the user's distinctive constraints.
rtf_to_skill.format: Return options with rationale, selection criteria, and refinement paths.

quality_matrix.label: Completeness
quality_matrix.repair: Name missing inputs, outputs, constraints, or success standards before deepening the package.

quality_matrix.label: Clarity
quality_matrix.repair: Replace broad verbs with observable actions and define what done means.

quality_matrix.label: Consistency
quality_matrix.repair: Check that role, task, format, exclusions, and examples do not contradict each other.

quality_matrix.label: Practicality
quality_matrix.repair: Add runnable steps, examples, or verification cues instead of abstract advice.

quality_matrix.label: Specificity
quality_matrix.repair: Anchor wording in the user's audience, domain nouns, and target outcome.

self_repair_checks: Check explicit need, implicit need, scenario, user level, and success standard before deepening.
Map Role, Task, and Format into skill behavior, not decorative prompt labels.
Ask one focused clarification only when missing information changes the package boundary.
Add tests or examples for prompt-heavy behavior before treating it as reusable.
Keep prompt methodology in references and reports instead of bloating SKILL.md.
prompt_quality.reviewer_note: Use this profile when the package depends on prompt behavior, role design, output contracts, or conversation quality.

checks.label: Conflict handling
checks.detail: The stated preference leans lightweight or speed-first, while the benchmark mix leans toward governance, review, or heavier evaluation structure.

checks.label: Artifact design profile
checks.detail: Execution-focused technical artifact with environment assumptions, copyable commands, expected outputs, and side effects made explicit.

evidence_readiness.reviewer_note: Use this section to decide whether the package is ready to deepen or should stay in discovery.
